title: Enable ongoing instrumentation using Instrumentation Hub

blocks.content:
Welcome to the Grafana learning path that teaches you how to scale instrumentation across your Kubernetes environment with automatic service capture.

In a real environment, you likely have dozens or hundreds of services, and new ones deploy regularly. Without automation, your observability coverage develops gaps. Ongoing instrumentation solves this by automatically capturing new services as they appear—when enabled for a namespace, any new deployment is detected by Alloy and instrumented with your configured settings.

![Instrumentation Hub showing discovered services](https://grafana.com/media/docs/grafana-cloud/instrumentation/ihub-first-page.png)

## Here's what to expect

When you complete this learning path, you'll be able to:

- Understand why scaling instrumentation is essential for growing environments
- Navigate to namespace settings in Instrumentation Hub
- Enable ongoing instrumentation for automatic service capture
- Verify that the setting is correctly applied

## Before you begin

Before you enable ongoing instrumentation, ensure that you have:

- A Grafana Cloud account. To create an account, refer to [Grafana Cloud](https://grafana.com/signup/cloud/connect-account).
- Grafana Alloy installed and connected to your Kubernetes cluster via Instrumentation Hub
- At least one namespace with instrumented services (complete the [Discover and instrument your first service](grafana://pathfinder/instrumentation-hub-discover-lj) learning path first)
- Access to the Grafana Cloud environment where Instrumentation Hub is configured

## Troubleshooting

If you get stuck, we've got your back! Where appropriate, troubleshooting information is just a click away.

## More to explore

We understand you might want to explore other capabilities not strictly on this path. We'll provide you opportunities where it makes sense.
